questions.question: What is the most important long-term maintenance strategy for managing social anxiety?

options.text: Avoiding all stressful social situations permanently
Taking medication indefinitely without any behavioral practice
Continuing to do the things you used to avoid and not letting avoidance creep back in
Only socializing with people you already know well

questions.explanation: The most important maintenance strategy is to keep doing the things you used to avoid. Avoidance can creep back in gradually. The lesson recommends maintaining social connections, periodically challenging yourself with new situations, and asking yourself 'Is this a genuine preference or avoidance?' when declining invitations.

questions.question: What percentage of people with social anxiety disorder also develop alcohol use disorder, and what is the typical pattern?

options.text: 5%, and it typically develops at the same time as social anxiety
Approximately 20%, with social anxiety typically preceding alcohol problems by more than 10 years
50%, and alcohol problems always come first
Less than 1%, and the connection is not well established

questions.explanation: Approximately 20% of people with social anxiety disorder also develop alcohol use disorder. Social anxiety typically precedes alcohol problems by more than 10 years. Self-medicating with alcohol increases the risk of substance use disorder by 2.5-5 times, creating a dangerous dependency cycle.

questions.question: By how much does social anxiety disorder increase the risk of developing depression?

options.text: It has no significant effect on depression risk
1.5 times the normal risk
3.5 times, with 35-70% of clinical SAD patients having comorbid depression
10 times, but only in older adults

questions.explanation: Social anxiety disorder increases the risk of developing depression by 3.5 times. Between 35-70% of clinical SAD patients have comorbid depression. The combination of SAD and depression creates a 6 times increased risk of suicide attempts, making early treatment of both conditions critically important.

questions.question: Which of the following is a sign that you should seek professional help rather than relying on self-help alone?

options.text: Experiencing mild nervousness before a presentation
Having occasional setbacks during your recovery process
Symptoms persisting despite 6-8 weeks of consistent self-help effort, or avoiding work, school, or important relationships
Preferring to spend some evenings alone at home

questions.explanation: Professional treatment should be considered when symptoms persist despite 6-8 weeks of consistent self-help, when you are avoiding significant areas of life (work, school, relationships), when you are using alcohol or substances to cope, when depression symptoms develop, or when you feel hopeless about improvement. Seeking help is a strength, not a weakness.

questions.question: Why is group therapy considered particularly effective for social anxiety?

options.text: Because it is less expensive than individual therapy
Because it provides built-in exposure to social situations as part of the therapeutic process
Because the therapist can observe your social skills directly
Because it is the only format of CBT that has research support

questions.explanation: Group therapy is particularly effective for social anxiety because it provides built-in exposure — the therapy setting itself IS a social situation where you interact with others, practice skills, and face feared scenarios. Individual, group, and internet-based CBT are all effective formats, but group therapy has this unique advantage of inherent social exposure.

title: Long-Term Management and When to Seek Help - Quiz
